bodyHtml:
dayjs is making waves in the Tools ecosystem this week, gaining 0 weekly downloads. over 48.7k stars on GitHub and 2.5k forks, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

Key Features

  • Written in JavaScript/TypeScript for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify
  • Official website: https://day.js.org
  • 🏷️ dayjs
  • 🏷️ date
  • 🏷️ time
  • 🏷️ immutable
  • 🏷️ moment

Why dayjs Is Trending

The Tools landscape is constantly evolving, and dayjs has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 0 new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: 2KB immutable date time library alternative to Moment.js with the same modern API

If you're working in Tools, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

How It Compares to Alternatives

When evaluating dayjs, it's natural to compare it against established alternatives. Here's what sets it apart:

  • Performance: Built with JavaScript/TypeScript, it leverages modern language features for speed.
  • Community size: 48.7k stars indicate strong community trust and validation.
  • License: MIT licensing means no commercial restrictions.

Quick Start

Get started with dayjs in minutes:

# Clone the repository git clone https://www.npmjs.com/package/dayjs # Navigate to project cd dayjs # Check the README for detailed setup instructions

For full documentation and advanced usage, visit the official GitHub repository.

Final Thoughts

dayjs is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
